articles.title: Family Indoor Entertainment Market Rides Wave of Digital Attraction Investment and LBE Demand
articles.summary: A new market report highlights strong growth in the family indoor entertainment sector, driven by investment in digital attractions and rising location-based leisure demand. The findings reinforce that FEC operators who prioritize tech-forward experiences are well-positioned for continued expansion. Operators and investors should take note of the demand signals shaping capital allocation decisions.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Sees Worldwide Boom
articles.summary: A new industry report confirms the global location-based entertainment market is experiencing significant growth, creating opportunities for FEC operators, suppliers, and investors. The expansion reflects broader consumer appetite for out-of-home experiences that cannot be replicated at home. FEC businesses that adapt their offerings to align with LBE trends stand to capture a growing share of leisure spending.

articles.title: TrainerTainment Brings Bowling Center Growth Strategies to Bowl Expo 2026
articles.summary: TrainerTainment, powered by The GrowthPro Group, will exhibit at Bowl Expo 2026 in Nashville from June 28 through July 2, focusing on actionable growth strategies for bowling center operators. The appearance underscores how specialized training and sales coaching are becoming key differentiators for FEC venues competing in a crowded market. Bowling operators attending Bowl Expo should consider visiting Booth 654 for practical tools to drive revenue.

articles.title: Designing Immersive Family Experiences: Key Principles for Modern FEC Operators
articles.summary: Industry leaders at the Festival of Innovation 2026 outlined how FEC and attraction designers can meet evolving guest expectations, emphasizing active participation, immersive storytelling, and technology that serves narrative rather than standing alone. Food, retail, and premium upsells were highlighted as critical revenue drivers, while repeat visitation hinges on gamification and emotional connection. These insights offer a practical blueprint for FEC operators looking to upgrade their guest experience strategy.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens at Potomac Mills Mall in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park has opened a new location at Potomac Mills, bringing its indoor slide-based attraction concept to the Northern Virginia market. The opening is another data point in the growing trend of action-park FEC concepts anchoring retail mall spaces. Operators in the region and those tracking the indoor action park segment should note the continued expansion of this format.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Unlike Any Other Opens in Atlanta
articles.summary: A new adventure park described as unique in the country has opened in Atlanta, adding another competitive entertainment option to a rapidly growing market. The launch reflects the ongoing proliferation of experiential FEC concepts in major metro areas. Atlanta-area operators and those tracking the adventure park segment will want to monitor this new entrant closely.

articles.title: Plymouth, UK to Get Major New Go-Kart and Laser Tag Entertainment Hub
articles.summary: A large-scale indoor entertainment venue featuring go-karts and laser tag is set to open in Plymouth, England, signaling continued investor confidence in multi-attraction FEC formats in the UK market. The project adds to a wave of new venue openings that combine classic FEC staples under one roof. Suppliers and operators in the UK entertainment space should track this development as part of the broader regional growth trend.

articles.title: Multi-Million-Pound Entertainment Venue Opens at Brent Cross Shopping Centre in London
articles.summary: A new upscale entertainment venue has opened at the Brent Cross retail development in London, continuing the trend of major entertainment investment in UK shopping destinations. The venue adds to a growing roster of large-format FEC-style experiences targeting urban consumers in the London market. UK-based FEC operators and investors should note the competitive landscape shift this represents.

articles.title: Largest Indoor Slide Attraction in the US Positions Itself as a Family Tourism Destination
articles.summary: A new large-scale indoor slide attraction in the United States is being marketed as a serious competitor to established family tourism destinations, drawing attention for its scale and ambition. The project illustrates how oversized anchor attractions can reframe FEC venues as regional tourism draws rather than local leisure options. FEC operators and developers should consider how scale and destination appeal factor into their own growth strategies.

articles.title: High Tide VR Opens Near Bethany Beach, Offering Immersive Virtual Reality Entertainment
articles.summary: A new VR-focused entertainment venue, High Tide VR, has opened near Bethany Beach, Delaware, catering to visitors seeking immersive out-of-home digital experiences. The opening highlights the continued viability of standalone VR attraction concepts in tourist and resort markets. FEC operators exploring VR as an attraction category can use this as a case study in positioning and location strategy.

articles.title: Lululand Adventure Park Announces First Pennsylvania Location
articles.summary: Lululand Adventure Park is set to make its Pennsylvania debut, expanding the indoor adventure park brand into a new state market. The announcement reflects the ongoing geographic rollout of branded adventure park concepts across the US. FEC operators and real estate investors in Pennsylvania should monitor this new competitive entry.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Offers Free Admission on International Day of Play, Launches Charity Fundraiser
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese partnered with The Toy Foundation to celebrate International Day of Play by offering free Adventure Zone admission and launching a June fundraiser benefiting children in need. The promotion demonstrates how major FEC brands are leveraging cause-marketing and community events to drive foot traffic and brand affinity. Independent FEC operators can draw inspiration from this model for their own community engagement programming.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Facility Set to Open in Springfield, Illinois in July
articles.summary: A new entertainment facility is scheduled to open in Springfield, Illinois in July, adding to the local family leisure market. The opening is indicative of the continued grassroots expansion of FEC-style venues in mid-sized US cities. Regional operators and suppliers should track new venue openings in the Midwest as indicators of market growth.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Venue Coming Soon to Newburgh, New York
articles.summary: A new entertainment destination described as a hot spot for guests of all ages is preparing to open in Newburgh, New York. The venue adds to the expanding FEC footprint in the Hudson Valley region and reflects sustained consumer demand for family-oriented out-of-home entertainment. Local operators should prepare for new competition while tracking what concepts resonate with this regional market.

articles.title: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth Announces Closure After Three Decades
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth, Texas has announced it will permanently close after approximately 30 years in operation. The closure is a sobering reminder of the ongoing challenges facing legacy FEC operators competing against newer, larger entertainment concepts. Industry stakeholders should study what factors contributed to this closure as a cautionary lesson in long-term venue relevance.

articles.title: Fort Worth's Tornado Terry's to Shut Down After 30 Years in Business
articles.summary: The family-owned Tornado Terry's amusement center in Fort Worth is closing after three decades, marking the end of a local entertainment institution. The story highlights the difficulty older, independently operated FEC venues face when competing with modern multi-attraction facilities. This closure serves as a case study for the industry on adapting business models to survive in an increasingly competitive landscape.

articles.title: New Bear River Entertainment Zone Draws Business Community Interest in Northern California
articles.summary: Chamber members from Fortuna and Eureka toured the new Bear River entertainment zone, signaling local business and civic enthusiasm for the facility's economic potential. The venue represents the kind of community-anchored FEC development that can generate broad local support and repeat visitation. Operators considering new venue development should note how business community engagement can build early momentum.

articles.title: Six Flags Great Adventure Launches Boardwalk Nights Summer Entertainment Event
articles.summary: Six Flags Great Adventure has debuted Boardwalk Nights, a new seasonal evening entertainment event at Shoreline Pier running select nights through early August. The program is designed to drive late-day attendance and extend guest dwell time through live entertainment and an elevated atmosphere. FEC operators can draw lessons from this event-driven approach to filling shoulder hours and boosting per-cap spending.

articles.title: Kings Dominion Elevates Summer 2026 with Cirque Show, Interactive Water Parade
articles.summary: Kings Dominion is expanding its summer entertainment lineup with the launch of Cirque Imagine and a new interactive Splash! Water Parade, placing live performance at the center of the guest experience. The strategy reflects a broader industry trend of parks using live entertainment to differentiate and drive visitation beyond thrill rides. FEC operators can take note of how layered programming throughout the day creates stronger guest engagement.

articles.title: Canada's Wonderland Expands Fireworks, Events, and Launches New Flexible Membership Program
articles.summary: Canada's Wonderland is boosting its summer 2026 offer with an expanded fireworks schedule and a new subscription-style membership program designed to increase frequency of visitation. The flexible membership model is a notable development for FEC operators exploring recurring revenue strategies. Membership and subscription models continue to gain traction across the amusement and FEC sectors as a tool for revenue predictability.

articles.title: Global Gathering of the Experience Economy (GXG) Relaunches in Madrid with Broader Scope
articles.summary: The former TEM conference has rebranded as GXG — Global Gathering of the Experience Economy — and will expand its focus to cover live entertainment, immersive formats, IP, venues, and cultural institutions. Produced by Fever, the event brings together over 350 senior leaders navigating the trillion-dollar experience economy. FEC executives and investors with an eye on trends in experience design and IP-driven entertainment should consider this event on their conference calendar.

articles.title: How Sound Design Shapes Guest Perception and Emotional Engagement in Attractions
articles.summary: A detailed exploration of how sound — even without visuals — can define a character, build anticipation, and create lasting guest memories, drawing on examples from Disneyland's history. The piece argues that thoughtful audio design is one of the most underutilized and cost-effective tools available to attraction designers. FEC operators investing in theming and immersive environments should treat sound as a primary design element, not an afterthought.

articles.title: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery Relocates from Holts Summit to Fulton, Missouri
articles.summary: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery, a small-format FEC combining arcade gaming and ice cream, has moved from Holts Summit to a new location in Fulton, Missouri. The relocation reflects the operational realities small independent FEC operators face in finding the right market fit and physical footprint. This story is a useful case study for independent operators weighing location decisions.

articles.title: Dataland, World's First AI Arts Museum, Opens in Los Angeles with Immersive Rainforest Experience
articles.summary: Dataland, co-founded by artist Refik Anadol, has opened in Los Angeles as the world's first museum dedicated to AI-generated art, debuting with a fully immersive rainforest environment powered by a large-scale AI nature model. The venue uses wearable biometrics and scent delivery to create a multi-sensory, responsive guest experience. FEC operators exploring cutting-edge immersive tech should study this as an example of how AI, wearables, and environmental design can combine into a compelling paid attraction.

articles.title: Inside Dataland: Biometric Wearables and AI-Responsive Art Create a New Guest Experience Paradigm
articles.summary: A first-person account of visiting Dataland in Los Angeles reveals how scent devices and biometric wristbands allow the AI-powered exhibitions to respond in real time to guests' emotional and physiological states. The experience raises compelling questions about the future of personalized, data-driven attractions in the FEC and immersive entertainment space. Operators and attraction designers should watch Dataland closely as a potential model for next-generation guest engagement.

articles.title: Universal Studios Hollywood Overhauls Guest Entry with New Security Checkpoints
articles.summary: Universal Studios Hollywood has reconfigured how guests enter the park with new security checkpoint infrastructure, likely driven by evolving safety and throughput requirements. Changes to entry flow at a major theme park carry lessons for FEC operators managing guest intake, queue design, and security protocols. Operators planning facility expansions or renovations should evaluate how entrance design impacts guest experience from the very first moment.

articles.title: Tivoli Gardens Opens Hikari, Its Largest Ever Themed Area, This August
articles.summary: Copenhagen's historic Tivoli Gardens is unveiling Hikari, a 2,000-square-metre Japan-inspired immersive themed zone this August, representing the park's most ambitious themed area investment in its nearly 200-year history. The project showcases how established parks can refresh legacy zones with deep cultural theming, atmospheric lighting, and immersive streetscape design. FEC operators and designers can draw inspiration from Tivoli's approach to reinventing existing spaces rather than building entirely new ones.

articles.title: Ultimate NOLA Golf Experience 2026 Highlights Growth of Entertainment Golf Concepts
articles.summary: A new entertainment golf experience in New Orleans is generating local buzz, reflecting the continued expansion of the competitive socializing and entertainment golf segment. This category — which includes concepts like Topgolf and smaller boutique mini golf venues — continues to attract new operators and consumers across US markets. FEC operators with golf attractions or considering entry into this space should track how these experiential formats are evolving.
